titleStmt.title: The Spanish Ambassador to the Secretary of State.

head.hi: The Spanish Ambassador to the Secretary of State.

div.p: Mr. Secretary. I have the honor to inform your excellency that according to reports I have received by wire from the Minister of Spain to Mexico, the revolutionists are laying, at Monterey and other places, as they have done at Tampico, on the Spaniards and Mexicans burdens from which other foreigners are exempt.
I am advised that around Monterey the Constitutionalists are pretending to make arrests with the purpose of demanding ransom from the Spaniards.

I bring these facts to your excellency’s knowledge and again call your attention to the discrimination shown against my fellow countrymen by the Constitutionalists, for which treatment there is no warrant as the Spaniards, as I have repeatedly had the honor to tell your excellency, have not meddled in the external politics of Mexico, nor am I informed that there is any proof of their guilt in that respect.

I therefore once more ask your excellency kindly to use your good offices toward remedying a state of affairs that is so unjustified and is causing serious losses to the Spaniards whose conduct entitles them to the same respect as is shown to other foreigners and avail [etc.]
